title: Read existing env before editing
objective: Understand the current 3D drone environment, reward function, render code, scripts, and tests before changing behavior.
codex_instruction: Inspect the existing environment implementation and identify the exact files that define reset, step, reward, termination, rendering, scripts, and tests. Do not rewrite working code unnecessarily.
files_to_touch: No edits unless adding notes to planning/status file
acceptance_criteria: Codex can summarize current env structure and knows where interception, reward, observation, and rendering logic live.
notes: Inspected env, geometry, rendering, scripts, tests, README, and acceptance files before editing.

title: Add viewport and fly-through config
objective: Add centralized tunable config values for camera FOV, detection range, target lock behavior, and fly-through success.
codex_instruction: Add config fields without scattering magic numbers. Include horizontal_fov_deg, vertical_fov_deg, viewport_range, lock_memory_steps, intercept_radius, flythrough_plane_radius, min_closing_speed, require_los_for_pursuit_reward, and require_flythrough_success.
acceptance_criteria: All new constants are editable from one config object or clearly marked config section. Existing defaults still run.
notes: Implemented in DroneIntercept3DConfig; check_env passed.

title: Track pursuer heading vector
objective: Represent where the pursuer camera is pointing so field-of-view checks are meaningful.
codex_instruction: Add a normalized pursuer_heading vector to env state. For the MVP, derive heading from pursuer velocity when speed is above a small epsilon. If nearly stationary, keep the previous heading. Initialize heading toward the target on reset unless randomization is already configured.
acceptance_criteria: Heading is always finite, normalized, and available in info/debug state. Stationary drone does not create NaNs.
notes: Implemented pursuer_heading state and info exposure; pytest passed.

title: Implement safe vector helpers
objective: Add robust geometry utilities for normalization, angle checks, projection, and line-segment tests.
codex_instruction: Create or update geometry helpers: safe_norm, safe_unit, angle_between_unit_vectors, point_in_cone_or_frustum, closest_point_on_segment, segment_sphere_intersection, and crossed_intercept_plane. Keep functions small and unit-testable.
acceptance_criteria: Geometry helpers handle zero vectors without NaNs and have tests for normal, edge, and degenerate cases.
notes: Added helpers and geometry tests; pytest passed.

title: Implement field-of-view detection
objective: Determine whether the target is currently visible inside the pursuer drone camera viewport.
codex_instruction: Add a function that checks whether target relative position is inside the camera viewport using heading, horizontal/vertical FOV, max range, and optional occlusion placeholder. Start with a cone approximation if rectangular FOV is too much, but name it clearly.
acceptance_criteria: info includes target_visible bool. Target in front and inside range is visible. Target behind pursuer is not visible. Target outside range is not visible.
notes: Cone FOV is acceptable for MVP. Rectangular camera frustum can be a future upgrade.

title: Add target lock memory
objective: Allow short-term target memory after the target leaves view so the policy does not become unstable immediately.
codex_instruction: Track last_seen_target_pos, last_seen_target_vel, and steps_since_seen. If visible, refresh memory. If not visible, increment steps_since_seen. Implement lock_memory_steps as a configurable value.
acceptance_criteria: info exposes target_visible, has_target_lock, steps_since_seen. Lock persists for configured steps then expires.
notes: This mimics a practical camera/track pipeline without implementing computer vision.

title: Add viewport-aware observation mode
objective: Make the agent search first instead of always receiving perfect target state.
codex_instruction: Add config observation_mode with values 'privileged' and 'viewport'. Keep privileged mode available for debugging/backward compatibility. In viewport mode, only expose target relative state when visible or locked; otherwise expose zeros or last-seen estimate plus visibility flags.
acceptance_criteria: Env passes check_env in both privileged and viewport observation modes. Observation shape is stable and finite.
notes: Do not change observation shape every step. Stable shape is mandatory for RL.

title: Add visibility features to observation
objective: Expose enough camera/search state for the agent to learn acquisition and pursuit.
codex_instruction: Add normalized features such as target_visible flag, has_target_lock flag, steps_since_seen normalized, angle_to_target normalized if visible/locked, and distance_to_last_seen normalized. Update observation_space shape and docs/tests.
acceptance_criteria: Observation documentation matches actual vector. Tests assert expected shape and finite values. check_env passes.
notes: This is where search behavior becomes learnable.

title: Replace distance-only capture with fly-through logic
objective: Success should mean the pursuer flies through the target, not just beside it.
codex_instruction: Implement fly-through success using previous pursuer position, current pursuer position, target position, intercept_radius, and closing direction. Use segment-sphere intersection around the target or crossing a small target-centered plane/sphere. Require that the pursuer path segment passes through the target capture volume during the step.
acceptance_criteria: A step segment that passes through the target sphere succeeds even if final position overshoots. Merely flying next to the target without segment intersection does not succeed.
notes: This directly fixes the current issue: flying next to the adversarial drone should not count as ideal success.

title: Add minimum closing-speed requirement
objective: Prevent slow grazing or hovering near the target from counting as a strong fly-through intercept.
codex_instruction: Add optional min_closing_speed gate for fly-through success. Compute closing speed along target direction. Start with forgiving default, but include config to disable if needed.
acceptance_criteria: Fly-through with sufficient closing speed succeeds. Same geometry with very low closing speed can be rejected when the gate is enabled.
notes: This encourages an actual intercept pass instead of slow side-hover behavior.

title: Add search/acquisition reward components
objective: Reward finding the target with the viewport instead of assuming perfect awareness.
codex_instruction: Add reward components for first acquisition, maintaining visibility, and reacquiring after losing sight. Keep weights modest so the agent does not stare forever instead of intercepting.
acceptance_criteria: info['reward_breakdown'] includes first_acquisition_reward, visibility_reward, reacquisition_reward. These are nonzero only under correct visibility events.
notes: Do not let visibility rewards dominate the final intercept reward.

title: Retune intercept reward for fly-through
objective: Make fly-through the dominant success condition while preserving current good training behavior.
codex_instruction: Rename or split capture_reward into flythrough_intercept_reward. Keep a compatibility alias in info if useful. Give the fly-through success a large reward and remove success reward from distance-only proximity.
acceptance_criteria: Distance-only near pass does not receive terminal success reward. True path-through-target event receives large positive terminal reward.
notes: Keep dense progress rewards, but do not reward ending beside the target as success.

title: Add aim-through-target reward
objective: Encourage the pursuer to line up its velocity vector so it passes through the target instead of sliding beside it.
codex_instruction: Add a reward based on predicted closest approach from the current pursuer velocity segment/ray to the target. Reward lower miss distance when target is visible or locked. Clamp it tightly.
acceptance_criteria: Reward is higher when projected path goes through or near the target and lower when moving parallel beside it.
notes: This is the main shaping term to fix the side-by-side behavior.

title: Add lost-target penalty
objective: Encourage the drone to search effectively and not ignore camera visibility.
codex_instruction: Add a small penalty when target is not visible and lock has expired. Keep it small enough that exploration still occurs.
acceptance_criteria: lost_target_penalty appears in reward_breakdown and triggers only when no visibility and no lock.
notes: This nudges search behavior without punishing necessary turns too hard.

title: Update reward breakdown schema
objective: Make reward debugging explicit and stable.
codex_instruction: Update info['reward_breakdown'] to include old core rewards plus new viewport/fly-through components. Add tests to ensure all expected keys exist.
acceptance_criteria: Reward breakdown contains all expected keys every step. No conditional missing keys.
notes: Stable info keys make debugging training curves much easier.

title: Render camera viewport
objective: Visualize what the pursuer drone can currently see.
codex_instruction: Extend lightweight pygame renderer to draw the pursuer heading ray and approximate camera FOV cone/frustum. Keep it cheap and understandable. Do not add heavy 3D dependencies.
files_to_touch: drone_env/utils/rendering.py or env render code; tests if rgb_array exists
acceptance_criteria: Human render clearly shows pursuer, target, heading direction, and viewport cone/frustum. Existing render still works.
notes: This helps visually confirm search and target acquisition.

title: Render visibility and lock status
objective: Make search/acquisition state obvious during visualization.
codex_instruction: Add HUD text for target_visible, has_target_lock, steps_since_seen, distance, and flythrough_ready if implemented.
acceptance_criteria: Render HUD updates every step and does not crash in human mode.
notes: Debugging RL without visual state is painful. Make it visible.

title: Render fly-through event
objective: Make successful intercepts visually obvious.
codex_instruction: When fly-through success occurs, show a short visual marker or terminal HUD message. If rgb_array is implemented, keep it compatible.
acceptance_criteria: A successful fly-through displays a clear marker/message before closing or in final frame.
notes: Optional polish, but useful for demos.

title: Add viewport search demo script
objective: Create a simple demo that shows search, acquisition, pursuit, and fly-through attempt.
codex_instruction: Add scripts/viewport_search_demo.py. Use a simple hand-coded policy: if target visible/locked, fly toward target/last-seen; otherwise perform a sweeping search pattern. Support --render and target_mode options.
acceptance_criteria: Script runs without training and prints whether target was acquired, reacquired, and fly-through intercepted.
notes: This is a sanity check before using RL training.

title: Add fly-through regression demo
objective: Create a deterministic script proving beside-the-target is different from through-the-target.
codex_instruction: Add scripts/flythrough_regression_demo.py that runs two controlled scenarios: side pass should fail; direct path through target should succeed. Print outcome clearly.
acceptance_criteria: Script prints PASS for direct fly-through success and PASS for side-pass rejection.
notes: This protects against accidentally reintroducing proximity-only success.

title: Test target outside viewport hidden
objective: Verify viewport observation prevents omniscient target tracking.
codex_instruction: Add a test where the target is behind or outside FOV/range. Assert target_visible is false and viewport observation does not expose fresh perfect target relative state.
acceptance_criteria: Test fails before viewport logic and passes after implementation.
notes: This is the core search behavior test.

title: Test target inside viewport visible
objective: Verify target detection works when target is in front, in range, and inside FOV.
codex_instruction: Add deterministic test with heading pointed at target. Assert target_visible true, lock true, and observation includes target/last-seen info as designed.
acceptance_criteria: Target in front is visible and observation is finite/stable.
notes: This prevents broken FOV math.

title: Test lock memory expiry
objective: Verify last-seen memory persists briefly and then expires.
codex_instruction: Add test sequence: visible target creates lock, then move target out of view, step until lock_memory_steps expires, assert lock false.
acceptance_criteria: Lock state transitions are deterministic and match config.
notes: Useful for stable learning and debugging.

title: Test fly-through success
objective: Verify segment-through-target success condition.
codex_instruction: Add deterministic test where pursuer previous/current positions bracket the target sphere. Assert terminated true and flythrough_intercept true.
acceptance_criteria: Passing through the target capture volume succeeds even if final position is beyond target.
notes: This is the main new success criterion.

title: Test side-by-side pass rejected
objective: Prevent the exact failure mode currently observed.
codex_instruction: Add deterministic test where pursuer flies parallel/adjacent to target within old capture distance style but does not intersect fly-through volume. Assert no terminal success.
acceptance_criteria: Side pass near target does not count as fly-through success.
notes: This test exists specifically because the trained drone currently flies directly next to the target.

title: Run full env compatibility check
objective: Ensure Gymnasium/SB3 compatibility after observation and reward changes.
codex_instruction: Run the existing check_env script. Fix any shape, dtype, finite-value, reset/step API, or observation_space mismatch issues.
acceptance_criteria: check_env passes for the default config.
notes: Do this before trying training again.

title: Run full tests
objective: Verify no regressions from viewport/fly-through changes.
codex_instruction: Run all pytest tests and fix failures without deleting meaningful tests.
acceptance_criteria: All tests pass or unavoidable skips are clearly justified.
notes: Do not claim success unless tests actually ran.

title: Run rollout sanity checks
objective: Confirm behavior is learnable/debuggable before RL training.
codex_instruction: Run random rollout, manual policy demo, viewport search demo, and fly-through regression demo. Capture concise output summary.
acceptance_criteria: Scripts run without crashing. Manual/search demo can acquire target in simple modes. Regression demo distinguishes side pass vs fly-through.
verification_command: python scripts/random_rollout.py && python scripts/manual_policy_demo.py && python scripts/viewport_search_demo.py --target-mode straight && python scripts/flythrough_regression_demo.py
notes: This is the final practical check before starting training.

title: Document new task objective
objective: Make it clear that the agent now has a camera viewport and must fly through the adversarial drone.
codex_instruction: Update README or env docs with the new task definition, observation modes, viewport config, fly-through success, reward components, and commands.
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain how to run old privileged mode, new viewport mode, visualization, tests, and demos.
notes: Future you needs this when reward tuning gets weird.

title: Summarize implementation and risks
objective: Give the user a concise completion report.
codex_instruction: After completing tasks, summarize files changed, tests run, what passed, what could not be run, assumptions, and future TODOs. Be honest.
acceptance_criteria: Final report is specific and does not claim unverified success.
notes: The final answer should be practical, not fluffy.
